title: US Officials Allege SMIC Supplied Chipmaking Technology to Iran's Military
detail: Two senior US officials alleged that SMIC, China's largest semiconductor manufacturer, began sending chipmaking tools to Iran's military approximately one year prior — with transfers described as 'almost certainly' including technical training. The officials did not specify whether the tools were of US origin, which would constitute a direct violation of US export control and Iran sanctions laws. SMIC has been on the US Entity List since December 2020. If confirmed, the allegations would represent a significant escalation — potentially exposing SMIC to secondary sanctions that could restrict its access to global markets and cut off equipment imports from third countries. Japan Times and Taipei Times were among the first outlets to report the claims. SMIC did not immediately comment.

title: ByteDance Valuation Reaches $550 Billion Amid AI and Global Expansion
detail: ByteDance's internal valuation reached approximately $550 billion as of late March 2026, according to ChinaPulse, up from $440 billion in prior years. The company's portfolio has grown to encompass dozens of applications across entertainment, productivity, education, and enterprise software — with TikTok's US restructuring resolved and its global user base recovering. ByteDance has deployed approximately 500 Nvidia Blackwell systems (36,000 B200 chips) in Malaysia via Aolani Cloud, circumventing US restrictions that prohibit such deployments in China. ByteDance's 2024 annual revenue was approximately $155 billion, exceeding Netflix and Airbnb combined. The valuation underscores ByteDance's emergence as one of the world's most valuable private companies despite US-China tech tensions.
